mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a comprehensive enterprise Kubernetes platform that provides a developer-friendly environment for building, deploying, and managing containerized applications. It is ideal for organizations looking for a robust solution with integrated CI/CD, security, and monitoring features. Key trade-offs include the complexity of installation and management compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for interacting with the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

```

> This command downloads the latest OpenShift CLI and installs it.
commands: curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

steps.title: Create an OpenShift cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new OpenShift cluster with the specified version and node count in a given region.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=./cluster --log-level=info

```

> Ensure you have the OpenShift Installer binary and the necessary configuration files in the './cluster' directory.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures the kubeconfig file to allow access to the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=./cluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> This sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to the cluster's kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the OpenShift cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step configures additional features like autoscaling and monitoring in the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-operator.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-operator.yaml

```

> This command installs the Cluster Autoscaler.
> This command installs the Monitoring Operator.

uninstall.title: Remove the OpenShift cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=./cluster --log-level=info

```

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

kubectl delete pvc --all

kubectl delete service --all

```

> This command deletes all persistent volume claims.
> This command deletes all services.

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenShift cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your cluster and ensure all workloads are running correctly. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the status post-upgrade.

```bash

oc adm upgrade --to-image=<new-version-image>

oc get nodes

```

> Replace <new-version-image> with the image of the new version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig is not pointing to the correct cluster or the certificate is invalid.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KUBECONFIG environment variable points to the correct kubeconfig file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the namespace is incorrect or the resources have not been created.

**Fix:**
Check the namespace context with 'oc project <namespace>' and ensure resources exist.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that one or more nodes are not in a 'Ready' state.

**Fix:**
Run 'oc get nodes' to check the status and troubleshoot any nodes that are not ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the container image cannot be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the image exists in the specified registry.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy authentication methods and removed support for certain outdated APIs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 4.16, ensure to update your custom resource definitions to align with the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
